expectations.text: Contains a concrete analogy using familiar real-world concepts
expectations.evidence: Puppy learning tricks analogy — treats for correct behavior, improving over time.

expectations.text: Explicitly states where the analogy breaks down
expectations.evidence: No mention of analogy limitations. Doesn't note that ML models lack common sense or rich understanding.

expectations.text: Includes a visual (tldraw, Mermaid, or structured)
The visual teaches something the text alone does not

expectations.evidence: Starts with analogy, then examples, then 'why it's a big deal', then types. The one-sentence summary is at the END, not the beginning. No clear simple→complex progression.

expectations.text: Includes curated external links with descriptions
Technical terms introduced only after being tied to familiar concept
expectations.evidence: 'Supervised' = teacher with flashcards, 'unsupervised' = sorting Legos, 'reinforcement' = puppy treats. Well done.

expectations.text: No single section introduces more than 4 new concepts
expectations.evidence: Three types section introduces 3 concepts, each with its own numbered item.

expectations.text: Acknowledges that machine learning is genuinely complex/broad
expectations.evidence: Doesn't acknowledge difficulty or complexity. Presents everything as straightforward. No mention of nuance or caveats.
